name: Immune clearance of senescent cells surveillance pass for June 5, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Immune Clearance of Senescent Cells since the 2026-06-02 public baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Ran a delta surveillance pass from the 2026-06-02 immune-clearance bootstrap checkpoint through 2026-06-05. Targeted PubMed publication-date and Entrez-date searches for senescent-cell immune clearance, senolytic CAR T, uPAR, NKG2D, GD3, HLA-E/NKG2A, CMV, perforin, and immunosurveillance returned no direct post-baseline evidence that changes the public outlook. A broader same-window senescence/immunotherapy search found seven records, but they were wrong-scope or context-only reviews, cancer multi-omics, cardiac aging, ovarian cancer prognosis, or AML T-cell-engager resistance. The closest post-baseline primary source was PMID 42237538, which uses anti-uPAR CAR-directed fratricide to enrich engineered T-cell products during manufacturing; it is relevant to cell-therapy manufacturing context but does not test senescent-cell clearance, aging, human senescence target engagement, or a therapeutic senolytic intervention. ClinicalTrials.gov, bioRxiv, medRxiv, correction/retraction, and web checks did not identify a material public delta. No public outlook, source, study, finding, activity, bundle, or coverage assessment update is warranted.

materiality_decision.rationale: The public baseline already frames immune clearance as low-confidence animal_signal with accelerating momentum but no treated-human aging evidence. The post-baseline anti-uPAR manufacturing paper is adjacent to CAR T production and manufacturing constraints, but it does not alter stage, confidence, momentum, blockers, safety boundaries, or support-map evidence for immune-mediated senescent-cell clearance.

search_log.query: (senescent cell OR senescent cells OR senescence) AND (CAR T OR CAR-T OR NKG2D OR uPAR OR urokinase plasminogen activator receptor OR GD3 OR HLA-E OR NKG2A OR CMV OR perforin OR immunosurveillance OR immune clearance), publication and Entrez dates 2026-06-02 to 2026-06-05
search_log.summary: Returned zero records in both same-window publication-date and Entrez-date checks.

search_log.query: (senescent cell OR senescent cells OR senescence) AND (immunotherapy OR immune surveillance OR immunosurveillance OR CAR T OR CAR-T OR checkpoint), publication and Entrez dates 2026-06-02 to 2026-06-05
search_log.summary: Returned seven Entrez-date records and three publication-date records. None supplied immune-mediated senescent-cell clearance evidence or treated-human aging target engagement; close records are documented in excluded_sources.

search_log.query: (uPAR OR urokinase plasminogen activator receptor) AND (CAR T OR CAR-T), publication year 2026
search_log.summary: Returned six 2026 records. The already staged intestinal-aging source PMID 41291258 remains the direct aging-relevant source. PMID 42237538 is post-baseline but concerns T-cell product manufacturing; other uPAR CAR T hits are pre-window oncology or broad-review context.

search_log.query: (senescent cell OR senescence) AND (CAR T OR CAR-T OR uPAR OR NKG2D OR GD3 OR NKG2A OR HLA-E OR immunotherapy)
search_log.summary: Returned allergy, oncology, autoimmune, and generic immunotherapy records, including one NSCLC epigenetic-immunotherapy trial updated on 2026-06-05. No record tested senescent-cell immune clearance in aging or posted relevant results.

search_log.query: NKG2D CAR T and uPAR CAR T
search_log.summary: NKG2D CAR T searches returned oncology trials with no senescent-cell or aging indication and no posted aging-relevant results. uPAR CAR T and senolytic CAR T searches returned no direct trial records.

search_log.query: Date window 2026-06-02 to 2026-06-05 with senescent, senescence, CAR T, uPAR, NKG2D, GD3, HLA-E, NKG2A, perforin, immunosurveillance, immune clearance, and senolytic screens
search_log.summary: bioRxiv returned no matching records. medRxiv returned one false-positive neurology record that did not involve senescent-cell immune clearance.

search_log.query: Baseline support PMIDs 32555459, 38267706, 41291258, 37585504, 30575733, 31160572, 39730825, and 37001502 with retraction, erratum, or expression-of-concern publication types
search_log.summary: Returned zero correction, retraction, erratum, or expression-of-concern records for the eight supporting baseline PMIDs.

search_log.query: senescent cells CAR T 2026 uPAR NKG2D; immune clearance senescent cells 2026; uPAR CAR T senescent cells clinical trial; Deciduous Therapeutics senescent cells 2026
search_log.summary: Returned baseline sources, pre-window uPAR oncology/CAR T and review context, non-primary explainers, and no direct post-baseline primary source that changes public interpretation.

excluded_sources.title: Directing fratricide within T cell products using an anti-uPAR chimeric antigen receptor to drive the production of potent therapeutic cells.
excluded_sources.reason: Post-baseline primary source using anti-uPAR CAR-directed fratricide to enrich engineered T-cell products during manufacturing. It does not test senescent-cell clearance, aging biology, human senescence target engagement, or therapeutic senolytic efficacy.

excluded_sources.title: A convergent uPAR-positive tumor ecosystem creates broad vulnerability to CAR T cell therapy.
excluded_sources.reason: Pre-window oncology source about uPAR-positive tumor and stromal targeting. It may inform target-selectivity context but is not a senescent-cell aging intervention or post-baseline delta.

excluded_sources.title: Emerging strategies in senotherapeutics: from broad-spectrum senolysis to precision reprogramming.
excluded_sources.reason: Pre-window broad review covering immune-based senolysis, CAR T, GD3, and other next-generation senotherapeutic strategies. It does not add post-baseline primary evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Characteristic Changes in the Immune Landscape of Aging Liver: From Basic Mechanism to Clinical Transformation.
excluded_sources.reason: Aging-liver immune-landscape review; relevant to immunosenescence background but not senescent-cell immune-clearance intervention evidence.

excluded_sources.title: The impact of aging on antitumor immunity and cancer immunotherapy.
excluded_sources.reason: Broad review of aging and antitumor immunity; does not report immune-mediated clearance of senescent cells or a treated-human aging result.

excluded_sources.title: TP53 deficiency in AML induces resistance to T-cell engagers through an immunosuppressive secretome.
excluded_sources.reason: AML T-cell engager resistance and T-cell senescent transcriptional profile; not senescent-cell clearance or aging intervention evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Hepatic Artery Transfusion of NKG2D CAR-NK Cells Followed by Intravenous Infusion of NKG2D CAR-T Cells to Treat Patients With Advanced Solid Tumors With Liver Metastases Who Have Failed Standard Treatments
excluded_sources.reason: NKG2D CAR-NK/CAR-T oncology trial with no senescent-cell or aging indication and no posted results.

excluded_sources.title: Neoadjuvant Inhaled Azacytidine With Platinum-Based Chemotherapy and Durvalumab (MEDI4736) - a Combined Epigenetic-Immunotherapy (AZA-AEGEAN) Regimen for Operable Early-Stage Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC)
excluded_sources.reason: Registry updated on 2026-06-05 but is an NSCLC epigenetic-immunotherapy trial, not immune-mediated senescent-cell clearance.

notes: Triage selected this track as the next distinct surveillance target after the niche rejuvenation and support no-op pass.
No coverage assessment exists yet for this track; this ordinary surveillance pass did not discover a material category-level change requiring immediate coverage repair.

The public boundary remains unchanged: uPAR CAR T, NKG2D-CAR T, and anti-GD3 intervention evidence is animal or nonhuman-primate level; human HLA-E/NKG2A and CMV-CD4 evidence remains mechanistic or ex vivo; no treated-human aging trial was found.
next_actions: Continue ordinary surveillance for registered or published human senescent-cell immune-clearance studies.
Backfill a coverage assessment as a separate maintenance task if internal source-landscape completeness is prioritized.

Create a public bundle only if future evidence changes treated-human target engagement, functional benefit, safety, target-selectivity, manufacturing, persistence, or beneficial-senescence boundaries.
